message:
Replace all @ts-ignore with proper types in BeadsView

Comprehensive type-safety overhaul of the force-graph beads
viewer, eliminating every @ts-ignore and `any` annotation.

New type definitions added at module scope:
- SqlJsDatabase, SqlJsStatic: typed interfaces for the sql.js
  library loaded via <Script>, covering exec() return shape
- ForceGraphNode, ForceGraphLink: node/link data shapes with
  optional x/y for physics-engine-assigned positions
- ForceGraphInstance: full chainable API interface covering all
  ForceGraph methods used (graphData, nodeColor, nodeLabel,
  linkColor, linkDirectionalParticles, width, height,
  nodeCanvasObject, onNodeClick, zoom, zoomToFit)
- ForceGraphConstructor: factory function signature
- Window augmentation (declare global): adds initSqlJs, d3, and
  ForceGraph to the Window interface so window.* access no longer
  needs @ts-ignore
- StatusStats: typed record for status count aggregation

New helper functions:
- asString(value): safely coerce unknown SQL column values to
  string (null → "")
- asNumber(value): safely coerce to finite number (NaN → 0)
- normalizeIssueRow(row): maps a Record<string, unknown> from
  SQL query results into a typed Issue object using asString/
  asNumber, handling optional fields (blocks_count, blocked_by_count,
  triage_score) with null checks

State typing improvements:
- db: useState<any> → useState<SqlJsDatabase | null>
- stats: useState<any> → useState<StatusStats | null>
- graphRef: useRef<any> → useRef<ForceGraphInstance | null>

Database loading (loadDatabase):
- config.chunk_count extracted via asNumber with validation
- chunks array typed as Uint8Array[]
- Error catch uses `err: unknown` instead of `err: any` with
  proper instanceof Error check for message extraction
- Removed all @ts-ignore comments from initSqlJs access

Data fetching (fetchInitialData):
- Status count iteration uses for..of with asString/asNumber
  instead of forEach with any-typed row
- Issue row mapping uses normalizeIssueRow instead of inline
  any-typed object construction

Graph rendering (renderGraph):
- Node/link arrays explicitly typed as ForceGraphNode[]/
  ForceGraphLink[]
- Links handle empty result (linksRes.length check) to avoid
  crash on graphs with no edges
- Canvas node rendering guards x/y as `typeof node.x === "number"`
  since ForceGraphNode.x is optional (undefined before first tick)
- All callback parameters remove explicit `: any` annotations,
  relying on the ForceGraphInstance interface instead
- ResizeObserver uses captured `container` const instead of
  re-reading containerRef.current in the callback
- Cleanup function added: disconnects ResizeObserver, nulls
  graphRef, and clears container innerHTML

Effect cleanup (renderGraph effect):
- Properly chains cleanup: clearTimeout on the delay timer, then
  calls renderGraph's returned cleanup function if it exists
- Returns undefined explicitly for all code paths

Zoom button handlers:
- Extracted graphRef.current into local `g` variable with null
  guard before calling zoom/zoomToFit, replacing the optional
  chaining pattern that was unsafe with the overloaded zoom()
  signature

Removed unused import: Cpu from lucide-react

Removed the setInterval-based polling useEffect entirely — the
Script onLoad callbacks and window global detection via the
declare global block make it unnecessary.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
